meta.description: Real-life Indian legal scenarios with applicable sections, step-by-step procedure, and practical advice. All verified.

scenarios.title: Landlord not returning security deposit
applicable_law: TN Tenancy Act 2017 Sec 5 (or similar state law)
procedure: Send a written legal notice via registered post demanding the deposit within 15 days.
Mention deductions you accept (for actual damage), if any.
If no response: file civil suit before the Civil Court / Rent Controller (max ₹3L in District; higher in Civil).
Or invoke RERA if it's a builder/registered project.
TN: TN Tenancy Act gives landlord 1 month to return deposit after vacate.
scenarios.tip: Always sign a written rent agreement with deposit amount, deduction conditions, and return timeline.

scenarios.title: PG/room owner not allowing girls or being abusive
procedure: Record the abuse (call recording, text screenshots) — Indian Evidence Act Sec 65B.
Send written notice to PG/hostel owner.
Approach local police station — file written complaint or FIR if intimidation/threat.
Local Municipal Corporation / Health Dept for unhygienic conditions.
Consumer Forum if you paid advance and got below-standard service.
scenarios.tip: Take photos of the room before paying advance. Save WhatsApp chats as evidence.

scenarios.title: Employer not paying salary or PF/ESI
procedure: Send written demand to employer (registered post / email).
File complaint with the Labour Commissioner office of your district.
EPF claim via EPFiGMS portal (epfigms.gov.in) — get UAN active and file grievance.
ESIC complaint via esic.in if ESI default.
Labour Court application under Sec 33C(2) within 1 year of due date.
scenarios.tip: Keep appointment letter, payslips, and emails. EPF claims must include UAN.

scenarios.title: Lost money to online fraud (Paytm, UPI, fake call)
procedure: IMMEDIATELY call Cybercrime Helpline 1930 (within 1 hour for chargeback chance).
File complaint at cybercrime.gov.in.
Inform your bank to freeze beneficiary account.
FIR at local police station / Cyber Cell.
Preserve all SMS, screenshots, transaction IDs.
remedies: Money recovery if reported within 24 hrs
scenarios.tip: Reporting within 1 hour is critical — RBI 'zero liability' framework only applies if you report quickly.

scenarios.title: Wife facing domestic violence by husband / in-laws
procedure: Reach Protection Officer / Service Provider under DV Act.
File DV Act application before Magistrate (Sec 12) — civil reliefs.
Parallel FIR under IPC 498A/BNS 85 + 506 (intimidation).
Call Women Helpline 7827170170 or 181.
One Stop Centre (Sakhi) for emergency shelter.
remedies: Residence order — right to stay in shared household
scenarios.tip: DV Act gives civil remedies WITHOUT criminal arrest. Use Sec 41A CrPC/BNSS — police must give notice before arrest in 498A cases (Arnesh Kumar 2014).

scenarios.title: Cheque I received bounced
procedure: Get bank's return memo (must be returned for insufficient funds, account closed, signature mismatch, etc.).
Send statutory legal notice to drawer within 30 DAYS of receiving return memo (via registered post + email + WhatsApp).
Demand payment within 15 DAYS in the notice.
If unpaid: file complaint within 30 DAYS of notice expiry before JM First Class court of payee's place.
Court will issue summons. Trial proceeds.
scenarios.tip: Strict timelines! 30+15+30 days. Missing any = complaint dismissed. Save the original cheque + memo.

scenarios.title: Road accident victim — claim compensation
procedure: File FIR at police station having jurisdiction over accident.
Get charge sheet copy + medico-legal certificate from hospital.
Compute compensation: lost income + medical + future loss + pain & suffering (Sarla Verma formula).
File MACT claim petition within 6 months (no upper limit since 2019 amendment).
Insurance company is the main respondent.
remedies: Hit and run: Compensation Scheme — ₹2L for death, ₹50K injury
scenarios.tip: Multiplier method per Supreme Court — age × future income. Save all medical bills.

scenarios.title: Defective product or fake delivery (Amazon / Flipkart / shop)
procedure: Contact seller customer support, record TID/ticket numbers.
Send written complaint via post or eDaakhil (edaakhil.nic.in).
File complaint before District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission within 2 years.
Pay nominal fee (₹100 - ₹500 based on claim).
Hearing usually within 3 months.
scenarios.tip: District Commission handles up to ₹50L claim. State up to ₹2Cr. National >₹2Cr. eDaakhil portal works fully online.

scenarios.title: Someone filed a fake FIR against me
procedure: Get certified copy of FIR.
Approach High Court for FIR quashing under Sec 482 CrPC / 528 BNSS.
Grounds: no offence made out, civil dispute disguised, malicious, etc.
If arrested: anticipatory bail Sec 438 CrPC / 482 BNSS.
File counter-complaint under IPC 211/BNS 248 (false charge) and IPC 182/BNS 217 (false info).
scenarios.tip: Bhajan Lal guidelines (1992) — 7 categories where HC must quash FIR. Engage criminal lawyer immediately.

scenarios.title: Want a divorce — what to do?
procedure: Mutual Consent (Sec 13B HMA): File joint petition in Family Court after 1 year of marriage. 6-month cooling period (waivable per Amardeep Singh 2017). Second motion + decree.
Contested: File petition citing grounds — cruelty, desertion (2+ years), adultery, conversion, mental disorder, etc.
Mediation usually mandatory before trial.
Maintenance can be claimed under Sec 24 HMA + Sec 125 CrPC.
scenarios.tip: Mutual consent is fastest (6-18 months). Contested can take 3-7 years.

scenarios.title: Child custody after divorce
procedure: Family Court petition under Sec 7 GWA.
Consider: child's welfare is paramount (Gita Hariharan 1999).
Custody types: physical, legal, joint, visitation.
Below 5: ordinarily with mother. Above 9: child's wishes considered.
scenarios.tip: Court always sees 'best interest of child', not parental rights.

scenarios.title: Want my share of family property
procedure: Try amicable partition with family — registered partition deed.
If denied: file partition suit in Civil Court of jurisdiction.
Get preliminary decree fixing shares, then final decree with allotments.
Daughters have equal share in ancestral property (Vineeta Sharma 2020).
scenarios.tip: 12 year limitation period. Daughters' rights apply even if father died before 2005 (Vineeta Sharma).

scenarios.title: Neighbour playing loud music / disturbing peace
procedure: First polite request.
Police complaint under Sec 268 IPC / 270 BNS at local police station.
Approach Municipal Corporation / Pollution Control Board.
Sec 133 CrPC / 152 BNSS — Magistrate can order removal of nuisance.
scenarios.tip: Day: 55 dB; Night (10 PM-6 AM): 45 dB in residential. Use a decibel meter app for evidence.

scenarios.title: Sexual harassment at workplace
procedure: File written complaint to Internal Committee (IC) within 3 months (extendable to 6).
IC has 90 days to complete inquiry.
Parallel FIR under IPC 354A / BNS 75 if criminal aspect.
If no IC at workplace (<10 employees): Local Committee at District level.
scenarios.tip: POSH applies to all workplaces — including domestic workers, contract employees, students.

scenarios.title: Eve-teasing / catcalling / public harassment of women
procedure: Dial 100 or 112 for immediate help.
File FIR at nearest police station.
Provide CCTV / mobile recording / witnesses.
Women helpline 1091.
scenarios.tip: Women police stations exist in many cities. CCTV in public transport is admissible.

scenarios.title: Want information from government office (RTI)
procedure: Write to CPIO of the public authority — name, address, specific questions.
Pay ₹10 fee (free for BPL). Indian Postal Order or cash.
Reply within 30 days. Life/liberty: 48 hours.
First Appeal within 30 days if no/partial reply (Sec 19).
Second Appeal to Information Commission within 90 days.
remedies: Penalty on CPIO up to ₹25,000 for refusal
scenarios.tip: Use rtionline.gov.in for online filing to central govt. State portals exist for many states.

scenarios.title: Loan recovery agents harassing me
procedure: Record calls and threats.
File complaint with the bank's grievance redressal cell.
Banking Ombudsman if no response in 30 days (rbi.org.in/cms).
FIR under IPC 506/BNS 351 for criminal intimidation.
RBI's Sachet portal (sachet.rbi.org.in) for fake loan apps.
scenarios.tip: RBI says: agents can call only 7 AM - 7 PM. Cannot visit your home unless invited. Cannot abuse or threaten.

scenarios.title: Police refusing to file FIR
procedure: Submit written complaint to SHO. Get acknowledgment.
If refused: send written complaint to Superintendent of Police (SP) under Sec 154(3).
If still no FIR: approach Magistrate under Sec 156(3) CrPC / 175(3) BNSS — Magistrate can order FIR.
Or file private complaint before Magistrate under Sec 200 CrPC / 223 BNSS.
scenarios.tip: FIR registration is mandatory for cognizable offences (Lalita Kumari 2013). Police cannot refuse based on jurisdiction.

scenarios.title: Alcohol legality in my state — minimum age, purchase rules
procedure: Minimum drinking age varies: 18 (Goa, HP, KA, Pondy); 21 (most states); 25 (DL, MH, PB, HR); Prohibition: BR, GJ, NL, ML, MZ.
Driving over 30 mg/100ml = MV Act Sec 185 (drunk driving).
Public drinking — local police act offences.
scenarios.tip: TN: Only TASMAC outlets allowed. KL: bars in 4-star+ hotels. Don't drink and drive — license suspended.

scenarios.title: Seen a child working in shop / factory — how to report?
applicable_law: Child Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act 1986 Sec 3
procedure: Call CHILDLINE 1098.
Online complaint at PENCIL portal (pencil.gov.in).
Police complaint + Labour Inspector.
FIR under JJ Act 75 (cruelty) and CLPRA Sec 3.
scenarios.tip: Children under 14 cannot work except family enterprise (non-hazardous, after school). 14-18 in hazardous occupations is offence.

scenarios.title: Family threatening over inter-caste / inter-faith relationship
procedure: Approach High Court for protection order (writ jurisdiction).
Approach SP for police protection.
Marriage under Special Marriage Act 1954 — 30 days notice can be waived for security reasons (post-Hadiya).
Safe house can be sought from State Legal Services Authority.
scenarios.tip: Shafin Jahan (Hadiya 2018) — adult's choice of partner is fundamental right. Lata Singh 2006 — no offence is committed by inter-caste marriage.

scenarios.title: Hostel / PG / shop refusing to rent / serve girls
procedure: Document refusal in writing (record audio if needed).
File complaint with State Women Commission / Human Rights Commission.
Civil suit for damages.
Article 226 writ petition before HC if state actor involved.
scenarios.tip: Article 15(2) bars discrimination in hotels, restaurants, places of public entertainment. Private hostels' 'house rules' must not violate this.

scenarios.title: Passport not being renewed by Police Verification
procedure: Track status on passportindia.gov.in.
Visit PSK / POPSK to follow up.
File RTI to ascertain reason for delay.
Writ in High Court if denied without sufficient grounds.
scenarios.tip: Sudesh Kumar 2008 — denial must be reasoned. Mere pendency of case cannot deny passport unless court directs.

scenarios.title: Medical negligence — doctor / hospital mistake
procedure: Get all medical records, prescriptions, bills.
Get an expert opinion from independent doctor.
Consumer Forum complaint (CPA 2019).
Complaint to State Medical Council against doctor.
FIR under IPC 304A if death — but with Jacob Mathew safeguards.
scenarios.tip: Jacob Mathew 2005 — prior expert opinion from independent doctor needed for criminal case against doctor.

scenarios.title: Got income tax notice — what to do?
procedure: Read notice carefully — note section, AY, response deadline.
Login to incometax.gov.in → e-Proceedings.
Respond within deadline — usually 30 days.
Hire CA / tax lawyer for complex cases (assessment, scrutiny).
remedies: Appeal to CIT(A) within 30 days of assessment order
scenarios.tip: Ignoring notice = best judgment assessment + penalty. Sec 143(2) = scrutiny notice. Sec 148 = reassessment. Sec 156 = demand.

scenarios.title: Tenant not vacating house after rent agreement ends
procedure: Send written notice to vacate (typically 1-3 months as per agreement).
If tenant doesn't vacate: file eviction suit in Rent Controller / Civil Court.
Grounds: rent default, sub-letting, personal need, change of user, etc.
Decree of eviction → execution → bailiff handover.
scenarios.tip: Don't cut electricity/water — illegal and tenant can file civil + criminal case against you. Take legal route only.

scenarios.title: Someone is stalking me (offline / online)
procedure: Preserve all evidence — messages, call logs, screenshots.
FIR at local police station — invoke 354D / BNS 78.
For online stalking, also invoke IT Act Sec 66E and 66C.
Apply for protection order under DV Act if relationship exists.
scenarios.tip: First offence: bailable; second offence: non-bailable. Take screenshots with date/time. Indian Evidence Sec 65B certificate needed for digital evidence.

scenarios.title: How to register marriage in India?
applicable_law: Registration of Births and Deaths Act
procedure: Hindu Marriage: After religious ceremony, register with Marriage Registrar of jurisdiction. Application + 2 witnesses + ₹100-500 fee.
Special Marriage Act: 30-day notice → no objection → marriage before Marriage Officer + 3 witnesses. Civil marriage, any religion/inter-faith.
Online: Many states have online portals.
Inter-religion: SMA recommended. Conversion not necessary.
scenarios.tip: Compulsory marriage registration is mandatory in many states (e.g., MH, KA). Useful for passport, visa, insurance, succession.

scenarios.title: Cyber-bullying / online abuse / morphed photos
procedure: Screenshot everything with date/time.
Report on platform (Twitter/X, Instagram, etc.).
Cybercrime portal cybercrime.gov.in.
Cybercrime Helpline 1930.
FIR if serious.
scenarios.tip: Sec 79 IT Act — intermediaries (FB, Insta, X) liable if no takedown after notice. Forward emails to abuse@platform domain.

scenarios.title: Someone encroached on my land
procedure: Get land survey done by Revenue / Tahsildar.
Send legal notice asking to vacate.
FIR under IPC 447/BNS 329(3) if recent.
Civil suit for declaration of title + permanent injunction.
Or summary suit if registered title.
scenarios.tip: Adverse possession over 12 years can create rights for trespasser. Act fast.

scenarios.title: Passport impounded by police
procedure: Get show-cause notice; respond within timeline.
Writ before HC under Article 226.
Look Out Circular (LOC) can be quashed if grounds insufficient.
scenarios.tip: LOCs by ED/CBI without authority were quashed by Bombay HC in 2024. Maneka Gandhi 1978 — passport seizure must follow procedure.

scenarios.title: Tax on cash gifts / weddings / inheritance
procedure: Aggregate gifts in cash/kind from non-relatives in a year.
If > ₹50,000: entire amount taxable as 'Income from other sources'.
Gifts from relatives (spouse, parents, siblings, in-laws etc.) are EXEMPT regardless of amount.
Wedding gifts received by bride/groom: EXEMPT.
scenarios.tip: Maintain documentation — gift deed, donor's KYC. Inheritance is not taxable in India.

scenarios.title: Aadhaar card problems (name, address, photo)
procedure: Online update at uidai.gov.in for demographic details — ₹50 fee.
Biometric update only at Aadhaar Seva Kendra — ₹100.
Grievance: 1947 helpline.
Writ before HC if unjust denial.
scenarios.tip: Aadhaar is not mandatory proof of citizenship. Linking with PAN, mobile, bank is mandatory but limited (Puttaswamy 2018).

scenarios.title: Rape survivor — what to do?
procedure: Reach nearest hospital — medico-legal exam (MLC) MANDATORY and FREE.
FIR at any police station — Zero FIR concept (Lalita Kumari).
Statement before Magistrate under Sec 164 CrPC / 183 BNSS — by woman judicial officer if female.
One Stop Centre / 181 / 1091 for support.
Free legal aid via NALSA.
remedies: Compensation under Nirbhaya Fund / Victim Compensation Scheme (₹3-10L)
scenarios.tip: Identity of victim CANNOT be disclosed (IPC 228A / BNS 72). All-woman team should record statement. Recording in 'safe space' permitted.

scenarios.title: Want to stop child marriage in my community
procedure: Call CHILDLINE 1098 immediately.
Report to District Magistrate or Child Marriage Prohibition Officer.
File FIR — PCMA Sec 9 (male above 18 marrying minor) + POCSO if minor.
Court can issue injunction order under Sec 13 PCMA.
scenarios.tip: Marriage of girl <18 / boy <21 is child marriage — voidable at option of child. POCSO if marriage was consummated.

scenarios.title: Received fake currency note
procedure: Don't pass it on — knowing use is offence.
Take to nearest bank — fill 'Counterfeit Note Detection Receipt'.
Bank will impound and report to police.
FIR is filed by bank.
scenarios.tip: Banks DO NOT compensate you for fake notes. Always check serial number, security thread, watermark.

scenarios.title: School not giving Transfer Certificate / withholding documents
procedure: Written application with payment proof.
Complaint to school management committee.
District Education Officer / Block Education Officer.
Consumer Forum (education service) — CPA 2019.
scenarios.tip: RTE 16 — no school can hold back / expel up to Class 8. P.T. Munichikkanna 2009 — TC withholding is unlawful.

scenarios.title: Want to do court marriage — procedure?
procedure: Both must be 18+ female, 21+ male; unmarried; not within prohibited relationships.
Submit Notice of Intended Marriage to Marriage Officer of district where either has resided 30+ days.
30-day waiting period (waiver post-Hadiya).
Marriage before Officer + 3 witnesses + signing register.
Certificate issued.
scenarios.tip: Inter-faith / inter-caste — SMA is the secular option. Fee ₹100-1000 by state. Online filing in many states now.

scenarios.title: Crime happened in another city — can I file FIR here?
procedure: Yes — Zero FIR concept. Police of any station MUST register FIR for cognizable offence.
FIR is later transferred to PS of jurisdiction.
Especially for rape, kidnapping — mandatory.
scenarios.tip: Refusal by police is itself offence. Approach SP / HC writ if refused.

scenarios.title: How to get bail for accused?
procedure: Bailable offences: matter of right; bond + surety at police station.
Non-bailable: bail application before Magistrate. If denied: Sessions Court → High Court.
Anticipatory bail (Sec 438 CrPC / 482 BNSS) — before arrest if you fear false case.
Sureties — usually 2 with PAN/Aadhaar and property proof.
scenarios.tip: Arnesh Kumar 2014 + Satender Antil 2022 — bail is rule, jail is exception. Sec 41A CrPC/BNSS notice before arrest in offences up to 7 years.

scenarios.title: Family member taken away by police but no FIR — illegal detention?
procedure: Demand to see arrest memo + grounds of arrest in writing.
Police must inform family / friend AND allow legal counsel.
If detention continues unjustly: file Habeas Corpus writ in HC.
DK Basu 1997 — 11 mandatory arrest guidelines must be followed.
scenarios.tip: Arrest must be inside 24 hours; Magistrate's permission needed for further custody. Family must be informed.

scenarios.title: Business: Company not paying my invoice / vendor
procedure: MSME registered: file complaint at MSME Samadhaan portal — 45-day decision.
Otherwise: legal notice + Commercial Court suit if >₹3L.
If insolvency suspected: IBC Sec 9 Operational Creditor — National Company Law Tribunal.
scenarios.tip: Get Udyam Registration if MSME — significant benefits in recovery.

scenarios.title: Someone defamed me on Twitter / Instagram
procedure: Preserve screenshots.
Report to platform (Twitter, Insta) — Section 79 takedown.
Send legal notice via lawyer.
File criminal complaint under IPC 499/500 OR civil suit for damages.
scenarios.tip: Subramanian Swamy 2016 — criminal defamation constitutional. Truth + public interest is a defence.

scenarios.title: In-laws demanding dowry after marriage
procedure: Document demands — messages, recordings, witness statements.
DV Act 12 — civil reliefs.
FIR under DPA Sec 3-4 + IPC 498A.
Sec 304B/BNS 80 if death within 7 years of marriage with prior cruelty.
scenarios.tip: Dowry items (stridhan) belong to wife absolutely. Pratibha Rani 1985.

scenarios.title: Neighbour blocking my pathway / driveway
procedure: Civil suit for declaration of easement + permanent injunction.
If easement of necessity / quasi-easement claimed: prove with revenue records.
FIR under IPC 339 if wrongful restraint.
scenarios.tip: 20 years of uninterrupted use = easement by prescription. Documents help — photos, neighbours' affidavits.

scenarios.title: Father died without will — succession
procedure: Hindu: Class I heirs equally — spouse, children (including daughters per 2005 amendment), mother.
Apply for Legal Heir Certificate from Tahsildar / Revenue Court.
Obtain Succession Certificate for movables/securities from District Judge.
Mutation of immovable property in revenue records.
scenarios.tip: Vineeta Sharma 2020 — daughters have equal coparcenary rights even if father died before 2005.

scenarios.title: Want to make a Will
procedure: Write Will: identify testator, list beneficiaries, list assets, appoint executor.
Sign in presence of 2 witnesses (who must also sign).
Registration optional but recommended.
Can be revoked/changed any time.
scenarios.tip: No specific format required. Hand-written valid. Witnesses must NOT be beneficiaries. Probate may be needed in Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata jurisdiction.

scenarios.title: Got ED summons in PMLA case
procedure: Mandatory to attend. Non-compliance = arrest.
Take a lawyer (interrogation rules permit lawyer at visible but inaudible distance per Senthil Balaji 2023).
Carry ID, summons. Note who interrogates and what is asked.
Sec 50 — statements before ED are admissible in evidence.
scenarios.tip: Anticipatory bail can be filed parallelly. ED can arrest only with written grounds (Pankaj Bansal 2023).

scenarios.title: Friend posted fake ID — what's the offence?
procedure: Report account to platform.
FIR under IT Act 66C + 66D + IPC 419.
scenarios.tip: Even creating fake account in your own name to defame someone else = offence.

scenarios.title: Bank threatening to seize my home / SARFAESI notice
procedure: 60-day notice under Sec 13(2) — challenge within 60 days.
Sec 13(4) possession notice — file appeal under Sec 17 before DRT within 45 days.
Restructure loan via OTS (One Time Settlement) — request to bank.
Mediation / DRAT for appeal.
scenarios.tip: Mardia Chemicals 2004 — SARFAESI constitutional but with safeguards. Don't ignore notices.

scenarios.title: Family member mentally ill — what are their rights?
procedure: Right to access community-based treatment, medical insurance, confidentiality.
Right to make 'Advance Directive' (Sec 5) about how to be treated.
Independent Mental Health Review Boards exist.
Suicide attempt — decriminalized under MHCA 2017 + presumption of severe stress.
scenarios.tip: Mental Illness includes substance abuse. Restraint must be least restrictive. Hospitalization without consent — strict conditions.

scenarios.title: Got expired / fake medicine from pharmacy
procedure: Take photos of medicine + bill + batch info.
Complaint to State Drug Controller.
Consumer Forum for damages.
remedies: Imprisonment up to life (if spurious caused harm)
scenarios.tip: FSSAI for food. DCGI for drugs. Always insist on pukka bill.

scenarios.title: Wife filed false 498A against me
procedure: Don't panic. Section 41A notice from police is standard now.
Get anticipatory bail (Sec 438 CrPC / 482 BNSS).
Engage criminal lawyer.
Family Welfare Committee verification mandated in many HCs.
Counter-complaint under IPC 211 if proven malicious.
scenarios.tip: Arnesh Kumar 2014 — no automatic arrest in 498A. Police MUST give 41A notice first. Sushil Sharma 2005 — court must guard against false 498A.

scenarios.title: Internet shutdown in my area — legal?
procedure: Demand the order under RTI / court.
Writ before HC challenging proportionality.
Faheema Shirin (Kerala HC) — Internet access part of right to education.
scenarios.tip: Anuradha Bhasin 2020 — orders must be (a) written, (b) reasoned, (c) for limited duration, (d) reviewable.

scenarios.title: Pregnant unmarried woman — abortion rights?
procedure: Up to 20 weeks: 1 registered medical practitioner.
20-24 weeks: 2 doctors, for certain categories (survivors of rape, minors, disability, etc.) — includes UNMARRIED women per X v Principal Sec 2022.
After 24 weeks: medical board approval.
Confidentiality protected — Sec 5A MTP Act.
scenarios.tip: X v. Principal Secretary 2022 — marital status cannot deny MTP rights. Even minors entitled (without parent in some cases).

scenarios.title: Started earning from YouTube / Instagram — what tax?
procedure: Register PAN. Open business bank account.
Income from YT/Insta = business income (Sec 28).
TDS @ 1% under Sec 194O by platforms.
Gifts > ₹20,000 from brands: TDS 10% (Sec 194R).
File ITR-3 / ITR-4 (presumptive).
scenarios.tip: GST registration if turnover > ₹20L (₹10L for special states). Maintain books — bills, brand deals, expenses.

scenarios.title: My rights during arrest
procedure: Right to know grounds of arrest in writing.
Right to inform a family member / friend.
Right to consult lawyer.
Must be produced before Magistrate within 24 hours (excluding journey time).
Medical examination at time of arrest.
scenarios.tip: Arrest memo with witness signature. Female accused: only by female police officer.
